flashcards.back: Pathos (suffering) + logos (study) = scientific study of structural & functional changes in disease.[file:1]
Pathology: Structural (gross/micro) changes. Pathophysiology: Functional/biochemical derangements & homeostasis breakdown.[file:1]
Patient → Lesions → Etiology(WHY) → Pathogenesis(HOW) → Morphology → Dx/Prognosis/Treatment.[file:1]
Health: Complete environmental harmony (wide normal range). Disease: Pronounced deviation from normal.[file:1]
Combination of clinical features from altered physiologic processes ('running together').[file:1]
Sir William Osler (1849-1919): 'Practice quality = pathology understanding'.[file:1]
Rudolf Virchow (1821-1902): Father of cellular pathology.[file:1]
1. All cells from pre-existing cells 2. Disease = alteration of normal cell structure/function.[file:1]
Giovanni Morgagni: Clinicopathologic correlation via 700 autopsies.[file:1]
Scrotal cancer in chimney sweeps (Percivall Pott, 1775; soot carcinogen).[file:1]
John Hunter: Hunterian Museum (Royal College Surgeons).[file:1]
Karl Landsteiner: ABO blood groups (1900, Nobel 1930).[file:1]
George Papanicolaou: Pap smear cervical screening.[file:1]
Paul Ehrlich: Staining techniques + immunology (Nobel 1908).[file:1]
General (principles) vs Systemic (organs). Morphological vs Non-morphological.[file:1]
Histopathology, Cytopathology, Haematology (microscopy-based).[file:1]
1. Surgical (biopsy) 2. Experimental (animals) 3. Forensic/autopsy.[file:1]
Frozen section (Julius Cohnheim, Virchow student).[file:1]
1. Static (store-forward, sender bias) 2. Dynamic (robotic, real-time).[file:1]
Watson & Crick (1953); Human Genome Project 2003.[file:1]
ISH (localizes DNA/RNA), PCR (rapid amplification, Kary Mullis 1983).[file:1]
Separated medicine from religion; rational observation (Father Medicine).[file:1]
1. Blood flow changes 2. Vessel wall changes 3. Blood composition changes.[file:1]
Humoral theory, vitalism, spontaneous generation → now: biomarker fads, sender bias.[file:1]
Autopsy pathology (disease course reconstruction).[file:1]
Pott's carcinoma (soot); frozen section biopsy urgent.[file:1]
Addison's disease; serum cortisol → steroids urgent.[file:1]
Excisional biopsy (architecture > FNAC for Hodgkin).[file:1]
Virchow ('cells from cells').[file:1]
Rapid intraoperative Dx (minutes).[file:1]
Etiology→Pathogenesis→Morphology→Dx/Prognosis/Treatment.[file:1]
Sender bias (static imaging selection).[file:1]
1.Etiology 2.Morphology(gross/micro) 3.CPC 4.Molecular confirmation.[file:1]
Pathology bridges basic→clinical; essential for practice.[file:1]
Pathology = structural + functional disease study.[file:1]
Virchow revolutionized Dx (autopsy→biopsy).[file:1]
